bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Eugene Muhammad and Denise Garner
  • Has five siblings
  • Began fencing at age 13
  • Aspires to be the first U.S. athlete to compete at the Olympic Games in a hijab
  • According to her father, fencing was a sport that was uniquely accommodating to her religion, which requires her body to be fully covered
  • Interests include working out, camping, photography, politics and traveling
  • Three-time NCAA All-American
  • Muslim Sportswoman of the Year in 2012
  • Serves on the U.S. Department of State’s Empowering Women and Girls Through Sport Initiative
  • In 2014, launched her own fashion brand, Louella by Ibtihaj Muhammad, which she manages with her siblings
  • Works with the Peter Westbrook Foundation, an organization founded by Olympian Peter Westbrook to mentor inner-city kids through fencing
  • Growing up, she found inspiration from the Williams sisters and their confidence playing tennis.

USA Track and Field

Steven A. Mulhern

Quick Facts

Steven has two prominent family members that drive him to competitive excellence. His grandfather, Stephen Van Buren was inducted into the National Football League, Hall of Fame in 1965 and his brother John Mulhern was a Goal Ball bronze medalist at the 2004 Paralympic Games. Steven has had an interest in grappling since high school where he wrestled for Overbrook School for the Blind in Philadelphia, PA. He currently trains at the Blind Sports Organization and Liberty Bell Judo Club, both in Philadelphia, PA. Steven also participates in conditioning training at a local YMCA. To prepare for competitive events, he competes in local and regional able-bodied tournaments. His dream is to compete in the Rio 2016 Paralympic Games. He lives with his fiancé in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

Major Judo Awards:

  • 2015 Member, IBSA World Games, Seoul, South Korea
  • 2014 Member, IBSA World Team, Colorado Springs, USA
  • 2014Gold medalist, USA Judo, National Judo Championships for Blind & Visually Impaired, Reno, NV
  • 2013 Bronze medalist, IBSA Pan American Championships, Colorado Springs, CO
  • 2013 Silver medalist, Keystone Games, Harrisburg, PA (Able-bodied Event)
  • 2013 Gold medalist, USA Judo, National Judo Championships for Blind & Visually Impaired, Virginia Beach, VA
  • 2011 Member, IBSA World Team, Antalya, Turkey
  • 2010 Silver medalist, Philadelphia Cup, Philadelphia, PA (Able-bodied Event)
  • 2010 Bronze medalist, IBSA Pan American Championships, Miami, FL
  • 2010 Gold medalist, USA Judo, National Judo Championships for Blind & Visually Impaired, Dallas, TX
  • 2005 Bronze medalist, Keystone Games, Harrisburg, PA (Able-bodied Event)
  • 2005 Silver medalist, East Coast Championships, Greenbelt, MD (Able-bodied Event)

bio.education: Concord-Carlisle High School (Concord, Mass.) University of Minnesota ’23, Strategic Communications and Political Science
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Position: Second
  • Specialty: Women's Team 
  • Daughter of Anne and Matthew Mullaney
  • Began curling after watching the Olympic Winter Games Vancouver 2010
Biographical Information

Sydney Mullaney grew up in Massachusetts and first tried curling after watching it being played at the Olympic Winter Games Vancouver 2010. 

Mullaney was a two-time national junior champion (2020, 2021) and won bronze at the 2022 junior world championships. Mullaney was also a member of the U.S. team at the 2023 FISU World University Games in Lake Placid, NY, winning a bronze medal, the first-ever medal for Team USA in women’s curling at the World University Games. 

Off the ice, Mullaney graduated summa cum laude from the University of Minnesota with a degree in strategic communication and political science. She now works as a marketing communications specialist.

  • Switched to the Finn class before his first year at Stanford and steadily climbed through the rankings
